title: D2 wire-protocol headers spec
description: Single source of truth for all D2 wire headers. Codegen reads this and emits 4 per-transport catalogs (Common + Http + Amqp + Grpc) on each language side. Cross-transport entries appear in multiple per-transport catalogs at identical wire values, codegen-guaranteed; the Common catalog holds entries with multi-transport applicability for transport-agnostic consumers.

name.description: Wire-format header name. Identical across every transport listed in `applicability`.

constName.description: Public C# / TS constant identifier — UPPER_SNAKE_CASE.

applicability.description: Closed enum of transport bindings this header can appear on.

convention.description: Provenance of the header naming convention. Documentation aid; emitter surfaces in xmldoc.

description.description: Human-readable description of the header's purpose. Emitted as xmldoc / JSDoc on every catalog the header appears in.

deprecated.description: When true, the entry is deprecated: the generated C# constant + factory carry [Obsolete] and the TS constant + factory carry @deprecated. Additive — the entry is NEVER deleted. Deleting a deprecated (or non-deprecated) entry is a breaking change the JSON-diff gate fails.

deprecatedReason.description: Plain dev-facing English explaining why the code is deprecated; rendered into the [Obsolete("...")] message, the @deprecated JSDoc, and the changelog entry. NOT a TK key (developer-facing, not user-facing).

replacedBy.description: Wire code of the successor entry (e.g. RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND). Must reference a real, non-deprecated entry in the merged registry. Omit for pure retirement with no replacement.

sunset.description: ISO-8601 date for the future RFC 8594 Sunset response header. Edge-runtime-deferred: carried on the contract now, consumed by the Edge response middleware when it exists. Inert until then.
